pairs.question: What was the All In 2018 event, and who were its main promoters?
pairs.answer: All In 2018 was an independent professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event promoted by Cody Rhodes and The Young Bucks (Matt Jackson and Nick Jackson) in association with Ring of Honor (ROH). At the time, Rhodes and the Jacksons were part of the Bullet Club in New Japan Pro-Wrestling (NJPW) and ROH.

pairs.question: When and where did All In 2018 take place, and which platforms aired the event?
pairs.answer: All In 2018 took place on Saturday, September 1, 2018, at the Sears Centre Arena in Hoffman Estates, Illinois, during Labor Day weekend. The event aired live on traditional PPV outlets, FITE TV, and Honor Club, and was later made available for on-demand viewing on the NJPW World streaming service.

pairs.question: How many matches were part of All In 2018, and which wrestlers were involved in the main event?
pairs.answer: All In 2018 featured 11 matches, including two on the pre-show. The main event was a six-man tag team match where The Golden Elite (Kota Ibushi, Matt Jackson, and Nick Jackson) defeated Rey Fénix, Bandido, and Rey Mysterio.

pairs.question: What significant championship was contested at All In 2018, and who won it?
pairs.answer: Cody defeated Nick Aldis to win the NWA Worlds Heavyweight Championship at All In 2018.

pairs.question: What historical milestone did All In 2018 achieve in professional wrestling?
pairs.answer: All In 2018 was the first non-WWE or World Championship Wrestling (WCW) promoted professional wrestling event in the United States to sell 10,000 tickets since 1993.

pairs.question: How did the success of All In 2018 influence the wrestling industry?
pairs.answer: The success of All In 2018 inspired the formation of All Elite Wrestling (AEW) in January 2019. The Elite (Cody Rhodes, The Young Bucks, and Kenny Omega) became executive vice presidents of AEW, which later established All Out as a spiritual successor to All In.

pairs.question: What is the significance of All Out in relation to All In, and how has its status evolved in AEW?
pairs.answer: All Out was established by AEW as a spiritual successor to All In and was initially held during Labor Day weekend until 2024. After Tony Khan acquired ROH in 2022, All In was revived in 2023 as AEW's marquee summer PPV event, joining the 'big four' and becoming the 'big five' alongside All Out, Revolution, Double or Nothing, and Full Gear.
